you for your interest in UC Merced! </strong></p><p><br></p><p>For Admissions to the <strong>Literatures in English, Literature and Environment Emphasis, B.A.</strong> major, students must earn an overall transferable GPA of 2.4 or better, and must complete classes articulated with the following UC Merced courses by the end of spring term prior to fall enrollment or by the end of fall term prior to spring enrollment. For Admission purposes, all major preparation courses require a \"C-\" or better.</p><p><br></p><p><strong><u>REQUIRED</u></strong><u>:</u> Major Preparation Courses for Transfer:</p><p><br></p><ul><li>Complete at least two UC-Transferable courses in English, Literature or English Literature.</li><li>These courses may also fulfill the \"Additional Major Preparation Courses\" listed below.</li></ul><p><br></p><p><strong>Additional Major Preparation Courses:</strong> Recommended to be completed prior to Transfer</p><p><br></p><ul><li>Complete the following Introductory course: * ENG 010</li><li>Complete one lower division emphasis requirement: ENG 001, 019, 058-061 or 067.</li><li>Complete one lower division seminar course chosen from ENG 050-090.</li><li>Complete one additional lower division courses chosen from ENG 011-090 (One course may be substituted with a course chosen from ENG 105 -189 or WRI 025 or GASP 041A, please refer to the catalog).</li></ul><p><br></p><p>* Please note, in cases where a course is equivalent to both WRI 010 (College Reading and Composition) and ENG 010 (Foundations of Literary Studies), maximum credit is for only one course. The awarded credit will first default to WRI 010 as it satisfies an Admissions Requirement. Please visit the Prefix Agreement on ASSIST.org to view your college articulation with WRI 010.</p><p><br></p><p>Courses in the major must be taken for a letter grade and may not be taken on a pass/no pass basis unless the course is only offered on a pass/no pass basis.</p>","groupId":"8de26f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"MAJOR PREPARATION COURSES REQUIRED FOR TRANSFER","groupId":"cf607445-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":0,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p>Please read the <a href=\"https://catalog.ucmerced.edu/preview_entity.php?catoid=24&ent_oid=1064\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">catalog</a> page for more information about the <strong>Literatures in English, Literature and the Environment Emphasis </strong>major.</p><p><br></p><p>In addition to the Major Selection Criteria, all Upper-Division Transfer applicants must meet minimum <a href=\"https://admission.universityofcalifornia.edu/admission-requirements/transfer-requirements/preparing-to-transfer/basic-requirements.html\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">University of California admissions requirements</a>. Visit <a href=\"https://admissions.ucmerced.edu/transfer/requirements\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">https://admissions.ucmerced.edu/transfer/requirements</a> for more specific UC Merced admissions information.</p><p><br></p><p>Prior to Transferring to UC Merced, please be advised of the following for Junior Transfers:</p><p><br></p><p><strong>1) WRI 001 and WRI 010 are admissions requirements:</strong> WRI 001 is fulfilled by IGETC/Cal-GETC 1A - English Composition. WRI 010 is fulfilled by IGETC/Cal-GETC 1B - Critical Thinking/English Composition. Please scroll towards the bottom of the agreement to find the articulation for each course.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>2) Transfer Admissions Guarantee (TAG):</strong> UC Merced is one of the six UC's that offers Transfer Admissions Guarantee. Please visit the TAG website for more information: <a href=\"https://admissions.ucmerced.edu/transfer/tag\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">https://admissions.ucmerced.edu/transfer/tag</a></p><p><br></p><p><strong>3) General Education (GE Requirements):</strong> While general education is not required for admission, it can help speed up your time to graduation once you transfer to UC Merced. We highly recommend reviewing the <a href=\"https://catalog.ucmerced.edu/content.php?catoid=22&navoid=2163#transfer\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Transfer Students: Satisfying General Education</a> page in the catalog for a more extensive explanation of the requirements. Transfer students are strongly encouraged to complete IGETC in order to prepare for work within the School of Social Sciences, Humanities and Arts (SSHA). IGETC courses may double count with the major courses listed below. Please visit your Community College Counselor to learn more.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>4. This agreement displays all lower-division (or Major Preparation) courses required in the major.</strong> UC Merced courses on the left, approved (articulated) transfer courses to the right.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>5. Changes to this Agreements:</strong> Major requirements are subject to change from one academic year to the next. Newly-articulated courses are added on a rolling basis, and articulated courses can be revised. Visit ASSIST every semester for the latest information and consult with an Academic Counselor at your institution on a regular basis.</p><p><br></p><p>For more questions about admissions, please email: <a href=\"mailto:transfer@ucmerced.edu\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">transfer@ucmerced.edu</a>.</p>","groupId":"8fe26f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"SECOND COURSE IN ENGLISH
